mnemonics: RITUAL: Runnable Interface, Thread class, Using Executors, And Locks
keyPoints: Threads can be created by extending Thread class or implementing Runnable interface
Synchronization is critical for preventing race conditions in shared resources
The Executor framework provides a higher-level replacement for working with threads directly
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call start() method (using run() instead)
Not properly synchronizing access to shared data, leading to race conditions
Creating too many threads without using thread pools, causing performance issues
